# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees — Regular Meeting
### December 19, 2017

> **Note:** These are unofficial notes based on an auto-generated transcript of the board meeting. They may contain inaccuracies due to transcription quality. Please refer to official board minutes for the authoritative record.

---

## Public Hearing: School FIRST Report (Financial Integrity Rating System of Texas)

**Presenter:** Darren Rice

The district's School FIRST report was presented, covering financial data for the fiscal year ended August 31, 2016, and staff/student data from the 2015–2016 school year.

**Key Result:** Conroe ISD received a rating of **"A – Superior,"** scoring **100 out of 100 possible points** on all 15 performance indicators.

Highlights from the indicators included:
- **106 days of cash on hand** (90 required for full points)
- **Current ratio of 3.13** (3.0 required for full points)
- **Student membership increased 10.8%** over five years
- **Debt service coverage ratio of 1.84** (1.2 required)
- **Administrative cost ratio of 0.0402** — roughly half the acceptable threshold of 0.0855
- **0% variance** between PEIMS data and the annual financial report
- All pass/fail indicators passed (timely filing, unmodified audit opinion, no material weaknesses, debt compliance, timely payments to TRS/IRS/TWC, positive unrestricted net position)

The report and related disclosures are available on the district's transparency website.

**No public comments were received** on the School FIRST report.

---

## Call to Order — 6:07 PM

- A quorum was confirmed and proper notice was posted per the Texas Open Meetings Act.
- **Invocation:** Mr. Moore
- **Presentation of Colors:** Oak Ridge JROTC cadets — Commander Cadet Martin, Cadet Simon, Cadet Miracle, and Cadet Ames, led by Sergeant Twine
- **Pledges of Allegiance** led by Mr. Huber

## Citizen Participation

No citizens signed up to speak.

---

## Item 3: Consent Agenda

No items were pulled for separate discussion.

**Vote:** Approved unanimously (voice vote, no opposition noted).

---

## Item 4A: Capital Improvement Update

**Presenter:** Mr. Faulkner

- **Safety & Security Project:** Phase 2 completing over the holiday break. Phase 3 design work underway with security department and design engineers; board approval for Phase 3 anticipated around March 2018.

- **Grand Oaks High School:** On schedule for **August 2018 opening**. Practice fields, detention areas, driveways (connecting to the Grand Parkway), and exterior work progressing. Interior finish work proceeding from the top floor down (science labs on 3rd floor).

- **Katherine Johnson Clark Intermediate School:** Also on schedule for **August 2018 opening**. Driveways, building systems, interior partitions, and exterior finishes taking shape.

- **Conroe High School Addition/Renovation:** Central plant structure for mechanical systems nearing completion. Large crane removed; smaller crane finishing structural work. Second-floor concrete work planned over the holidays. On schedule; campus work projected through **December 2019**.

**Board Discussion:** A board member asked about potential Hurricane Harvey impacts on construction costs and labor availability. Mr. Faulkner reported:
- No significant Harvey-related shortages or cost impacts observed to date.
- Recent bids for a new junior high school received over 400 bids across 50+ trades, indicating strong participation.
- Drywall (a concern area) bids appeared unaffected.
- A similar-sized nearby district reported a comparable experience.

---

## Item 5A: Award of RFP 17-04-05 — South County Sports Facility Naming Rights

**Presenter:** Darren Rice

**Recommendation:** Award naming rights for the **stadium only** to **Woodforest National Bank** for **$1,000,100** (as stated in transcript).

- The previous naming rights agreement with Woodforest National Bank (approved February 2007) expires August 31, 2018.
- RFP was released October 18, 2017 via the district's e-bidding system, advertised three times in *The Courier*, once in *The Villager*, and on the district website.
- 113 vendors were invited; 3 responded (2 submitted "no bids," 1 submitted a proposal — Woodforest National Bank).
- Contract anticipated for up to 10 years with possible extensions.

**Board Discussion:** A board member asked about the low response rate. Mr. Rice explained that the 113 vendors were sent emails, and most simply chose not to bid. He noted a superintendent of a similar-sized district had a very similar response level.

**Vote:** Approved by voice vote. **One abstention** noted (member not identified by name). No opposition.

- Bank deposits: ~$2 million
- State pool investments: $55.5 million
- Short-term investments (<1 year): $59.4 million
- Longer-term investments (TCG Investment Advisors): $51.7 million
- **Total cash and investments: $168.6 million**

### Property Tax Collections
- Early in the collection cycle; on pace with the prior year.

### General Fund Projections
- Projected general fund balance: **$14.9 million** (preliminary; does not yet include transfer to debt service)

### 2015 Bond Referendum Status
- Total funding: **$520.2 million** (includes $33 million board transfer to capital projects)
- Project forecast: **$514.8 million**
- **Remaining contingency: $5.4 million**

### Self-Funded Insurance Plan

- Total expenses (YTD): ~$9.7 million

- Wellness center participation: 542 visits in November; 1,526 total YTD

### Investment Portfolio (November)

- Pool yield: 1.27%
- Short-term investments yield: 1.45%

- **Combined portfolio yield: 1.33%** (weighted average maturity: 98 days)

## Item 8A: Board Continuing Education Announcement

All members of the Conroe ISD Board of Trustees have **completed the training required by law** for the year.

## Item 8B: Local Policy Manual Update 109

**Presenter:** Gladys (General Counsel)

An informational overview of local policy updates resulting from the most recent Texas legislative session. Key changes include:

- **Bullying policy:** Expanded per state law changes
- **Senate Bill 7 (Educator Misconduct):** Extensive new requirements regarding reporting of sexual misconduct between educators and students, reflected across multiple local policies
- **Specialized camera bill (tweaked):** Modified definitions for who can request cameras in classrooms; video retention period reduced from 6 months to **3 months**

**No vote taken** — the board will be asked to formally adopt the updated policies in **January 2018**.

---

- No other final action was reported from executive session.

**Motion to adjourn approved. Meeting adjourned at 6:45 PM.**
